summary: Recent updates to Firefox have focused on improving the stability of audio playback in various scenarios, particularly when changes occur in playback rates. Specifically, the introduction of a crashtest helps ensure that the browser can handle adjustments to channel counts—referring to the number of audio tracks being processed—without crashing. This is important as it enhances the robustness of the media playback experience, particularly for technologies that rely on varying playback rates, such as streaming services that may adjust speed based on user preferences. **Improving stability in audio playback not only boosts performance but also supports a smoother user experience across a range of multimedia applications.**
